environment.hardware: No special hardware required. The computation is dense real-symmetric exact diagonalization by a hand-rolled cyclic Jacobi routine on matrices up to 256x256 (N=8 open-boundary TFIM chain); verified on Apple Silicon (aarch64) macOS 25.5.0 (Darwin), no GPU, cluster, or large-memory dependency. The LaTeX build is a standard single-machine pdflatex+bibtex compile (tikz-cd/tikz diagrams only, no external figure assets).
environment.software: GHC 9.14.1 (Homebrew) and Cabal 3.16.1.0 (Hackage index-state 2026-07-14T20:00:04Z) for the Haskell suite; pdfTeX 3.141592653-2.6-1.40.29 (TeX Live 2026) with BibTeX 0.99e for the paper build.
dependencies: base 4.22.0.0 (GHC boot package; TFIM.hs itself is base-only as its docstring claims -- confirmed by inspection, it imports only Control.Monad, Data.Bits, Data.List, Foreign.Marshal.Array/Ptr/Storable, and System.IO.Unsafe, no array/containers/vector/hmatrix)
QuickCheck 2.18.0.0 (resolved from Hackage; required transitively by Properties.hs and hence Main.hs, but not declared in any manifest anywhere in src/spectral-gap-stability/ -- see concerns)

concerns: src/spectral-gap-stability/ ships three bare .hs files (TFIM.hs, Properties.hs, Main.hs) with no .cabal file, stack.yaml, package.yaml, or README. This is notable because three of this paper's four sibling topic directories -- src/lieb-robinson-locality/, src/positivity-cstar-norms/, and src/lattice-eft-equivalence/ -- now ship a proper .cabal manifest declaring build-depends (including QuickCheck) and -Wall -Wextra -Werror ghc-options, while spectral-gap-stability (this paper, Part III) and bordism-realizability (Part V) do not. I had to construct my own scratch cabal project (hs-source-dirs pointing at the real source tree, build-depends: base, QuickCheck) to build and run the suite at all; a reader following only Section 8.5's pointer to the source directory has no declared, repeatable way to do this, and would have to discover the QuickCheck dependency by reading Properties.hs's import list.

Section 8.1 states that for the dimensions used (N<=8, matrices up to 256x256) 'the eigenvalues are accurate to near machine precision -- the identities (17) hold to ~1e-13 in the computation.' I independently measured the actual residuals by calling TFIM's spectrum/expectedTraceSq directly (not just running the QuickCheck suite, whose tolerances are much looser at 1e-6 absolute / 1e-5 relative): at N=4 the residuals are indeed ~1e-13 to 1e-15, matching the paper's figure well, but at N=8 -- the largest system size the paper says this claim covers -- the Tr H^2 residual reaches 3e-10 to 7e-10, about three orders of magnitude looser than '~1e-13'. This does not change any conclusion (the eigensolver is still highly accurate relative to the scales involved, and the actual QuickCheck tolerances are satisfied with wide margin), but the specific quoted precision is representative only of the smallest system size tested, not uniformly across the N<=8 range the sentence describes.

Minor: Properties.hs's prop_kwDuality tests a Kramers-Wannier-type duality of the exact free-fermion gap formula (gap(g) = g*gap(1/g)) that is not stated anywhere in the paper's text. This is in mild tension with Section 8.5's claim that 'the properties are stated one per claim and cite the corresponding equation or theorem, so that the executable tests track the paper's assertions' -- six of the seven properties do cite a specific equation or theorem (eq. 17 twice, Hamiltonian symmetry, Theorem III-A, and the two gap-shape claims of Section 8.2), but this seventh one tests a valid identity of eq. (8) that the paper itself never derives or mentions. Not a bug, just an uncited extra check.

No public code repository URL, DOI, or archive pointer appears anywhere in the manuscript; the code is reachable only via the shared development repository's src/spectral-gap-stability/ path used across this six-part series. Consistent with the rest of the series and not a claim the paper makes (unlike a sibling part, this paper does not assert the code is bundled with an arXiv submission or supplementary materials), but noted for completeness.
